food_subgroup: Fast food burger combo meal with large fries and large soft drink

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general hamburger class (FOODON_00002383); no specific Big Mac or combo-meal class exists. Mapped to closest parent class. The upsized meal (burger + large fries + large drink) is a composite product not individually represented in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FHR (Hamburger) is the closest defensible match. No specific Big Mac or upsized-meal composite code exists in FoodEx2. Nutrient values reflect the full upsized meal composite.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226789007 (Hamburger) is the closest available concept. No specific Big Mac Upsized Meal concept exists in SNOMED CT.

preparation_method: assembled with sauce, lettuce, cheese, pickles, onions on sesame seed bun

serving.description: 1 upsized meal (Big Mac burger + large fries + large soft drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One McDonald's Big Mac Upsized Meal consisting of one Big Mac burger (approximately 200 g), one large portion of french fries (approximately 154 g), and one large Coca-Cola soft drink (approximately 406 g / 400 ml), totalling approximately 760 g.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's Singapore Nutrition Information — Big Mac, Large Fries, Large Coca-Cola (composite calculation)
nutrient_source.organization: McDonald's Singapore / USDA FoodData Central (FDC) cross-referenced
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: McDonald's Singapore publishes per-item nutrition data. The upsized meal is a composite of: Big Mac burger (~200 g, per McDonald's SG nutrition panel), Large Fries (~154 g, per McDonald's SG), and Large Coca-Cola (~400 ml / ~406 g). Per-100g values are computed from the weighted composite total (~760 g). USDA FoodData Central (FDC IDs: 21237 Big Mac, 21142 Large Fries, 14400 Coca-Cola) used for cross-validation of macro ratios. McDonald's Singapore is a branded-food operator source; USDA Branded Food is an approved secondary source for commercial products.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimates for fast-food burger meal components
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the McDonald's Big Mac Upsized Meal as a composite. GI estimated as a weighted composite: Big Mac burger bun and patty component estimated GI ~65 (white wheat bun dominant, analogous to hamburger bun GI ~61 per University of Sydney GI Database); large fries GI ~63–75 (french fries GI ~63 per University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2008); large Coca-Cola GI ~63 (carbonated cola per University of Sydney GI Database). Weighted composite GI ~65 (medium). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 135.3) / 100 ≈ 88; rounded to 83 accounting for fibre adjustment. Carbohydrate per serving: 17.8 g/100g × 760 g = 135.3 g total carbohydrate. GL per serving = (65 × 135.3) / 100 = 87.9, reported as 83.0 after dietary fibre deduction (net carbs ~127.5 g: 135.3 − 6.1 g fibre; GL = 65 × 127.5 / 100 = 82.9 ≈ 83.0).

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content (~1482 mg per meal serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit consumption of this meal, as a single upsized serving provides approximately 1482 mg sodium (~64% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2300 mg), which can acutely elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high glycemic load (~83 per meal) and high refined carbohydrate content (~135 g per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this meal or consume it only rarely, as the very high glycemic load (approximately 83 per serving) from the combination of white-flour bun, large fries, and sugary cola will cause a substantial postprandial blood glucose spike and is inconsistent with glycaemic management goals.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat, trans fat, sodium, and total caloric density
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of this meal, as it delivers high levels of saturated fat (~16 g per serving), trans fat (~0.76 g per serving), and sodium (~1482 mg per serving), all of which are established risk factors for atherosclerosis and adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The McDonald's Big Mac Upsized Meal is a fast-food combo meal sold at McDonald's restaurants in Singapore, consisting of one Big Mac burger, one large portion of french fries, and one large carbonated soft drink (typically Coca-Cola). The Big Mac burger features two grilled 100% beef patties layered with Big Mac sauce (a creamy, tangy special sauce, served with 50% more sauce in this variant), shredded lettuce, one slice of American processed cheese, sliced pickles, and diced onions, all served on a three-part toasted sesame seed bun. The large fries are deep-fried potato strips, lightly salted. The large Coca-Cola is a sweetened carbonated cola beverage. The meal is certified Halal in Singapore. Nutritionally, the upsized meal is calorie-dense, providing approximately 1125 kcal per serving, with high carbohydrate content (~135 g), moderate-to-high protein (~42 g), and significant fat (~47 g including ~16 g saturated fat). Sodium content is very high at approximately 1482 mg per serving. The glycemic load is very high (~83), driven by the refined white-flour bun, large fries, and sugary cola. This meal is classified as ultra-processed and is of high clinical concern for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those avoiding gluten or dairy. The meal is widely recognised in Singapore as a popular fast-food option and is available at McDonald's outlets island-wide.

confidence_reason: McDonald's Singapore publishes per-item nutrition data on their official website, which is a branded commercial source. Per-100g values for this composite upsized meal are derived by weighted calculation across three components (Big Mac burger, large fries, large Coca-Cola) using McDonald's Singapore nutrition panels cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central branded entries. The composite calculation introduces moderate uncertainty, particularly for the soft drink component which varies by flavour choice. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component values. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. This record covers the McDonald's Big Mac Upsized Meal as sold in Singapore via field survey (McDonald's Springleaf Tower and Boat Quay outlets). 2. The upsized meal total weight is estimated at approximately 760 g (Big Mac ~200 g + large fries ~154 g + large Coca-Cola ~406 g). Per-100g nutrient values are computed from this composite. 3. Vitamin B1, B2, B3, B6, B12, folate, vitamin D, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, and selenium are set to -1 as these micronutrients are not individually reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel for this composite meal, and component-level micronutrient data was not available with sufficient precision to compute a reliable composite per-100g figure. 4. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, vitamin C, vitamin A, cholesterol, and water are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component cross-reference and are approximate. 5. GI of 65 (medium) is an analogous composite estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific meal exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. The soft drink component is assumed to be Coca-Cola (standard); substitution with diet cola or other beverages would materially alter sugar, carbohydrate, and GI values. 7. Halal certification applies to McDonald's Singapore outlets. 8. The description notes '50% more sauce' as per the field survey product description; this may affect caloric and fat values marginally compared to the standard Big Mac. 9. Price is fixed at SGD 11.40 per field survey listing (both outlets). 10. All -1 sentinel values are documented above per G3 requirements.
